Config.AlertSavedHtml: Configuration saved. Changes take effect after restart.
Config.DirectFailureFallbackHint: Temporarily route a domain through the proxy after repeated direct connection failures.

RuleResource.AlreadyLatest: Rule resources are already up to date.

Config.EnableLanProxy: Open proxy port in TUN mode
Config.ModeHintProxyHtml:
Local Proxy mode listens on a local port and can automatically update the system proxy. No administrator rights are required.
Use the status page to restart or stop the current service.
Config.ModeHintTunHtml:
TUN mode transparently captures TCP traffic through the virtual adapter. FakeIP DNS is enabled by default — the DNS proxy returns virtual addresses so domain-based routing decisions can be made before the real IP is known.
Use the status page to restart or stop the current service.
Config.PacDescriptionHtml: PAC (Proxy Auto-Config) decides whether requests should use the proxy or go direct based on domain rules. GFWList entries are merged automatically when enabled.
Config.PacSetFailed: Failed to apply PAC. Windows only.

ProxyStatus.Hint: Optional check through this proxy against Google, GitHub, and YouTube.
ProxyStatus.AvailableHint: Connection check passed. Save and restart to apply configuration changes.
ProxyStatus.UnavailableHint: Connection check failed. You can still save endpoint settings or download resources if the proxy is reachable for those requests.
ProxyStatus.RequiredBeforeSave: Enter the upstream proxy host and port before saving or downloading rule resources.

Config.RoutingLocked: Routing rules are locked until the upstream proxy check passes.
Config.SmartRoutingDescription: Smart routing evaluates enabled rules only when their files are present and valid. GFWList is evaluated first, then GeoIP.
Config.DnsCacheHint: DNS cache records can be viewed and cleared from the DNS page.

SaveStatus.EndpointRequired: Enter the upstream proxy host and port to enable saving.
SaveStatus.Ready: Configuration changes are ready to save.

Config.StepUpstreamHint: Fill in the proxy endpoint first. The connection test is optional and does not block saving or resource downloads.
Config.StepRoutingResourcesHint: Download GFWList and GeoIP resources before enabling smart routing modes that depend on them.
Config.StepProxyModeHint: Choose how TunProxy should take over traffic, then save and restart to apply the final configuration.
Config.StepAdvancedHint: Optional: configure the proxy listen address to enable LAN clients to connect for multi-hop forwarding.
Config.Title: Config

Dns.Empty: No records yet. Data will appear automatically once traffic is flowing.
Dns.Heading: DNS Resolution Records
Dns.ProxyModeNoticeHtml: Local Proxy mode does not need DNS interception. Browsers send hostnames directly to the upstream proxy. This page only has data in TUN mode.
Dns.EmptyApi: The API returned no records. New DNS/TUN traffic will appear automatically.
Dns.LegendDirect: Private IP, resolved private IP, direct domain, or direct Geo
Dns.Title: DNS
Dns.DoHNoticeBody: Chrome and Edge enable Secure DNS (DoH) by default. DNS queries are sent over HTTPS and bypass TUN's UDP port 53 interception, so they do not appear here. To see complete records, disable "Use secure DNS" in your browser's security settings.

Logs.EmptyHtml:
No logs yet. They will appear automatically after the service starts.
TUN mode: [CONN] shows TCP routing decisions and [DNS] shows DNS resolution results.
Local Proxy mode: [CONN] shows CONNECT and HTTP request routing decisions.
Logs.Heading: Live Logs
Logs.Title: Logs

Rules.Title: Routing Rules
Rules.ManualRulesHint: Proxy domains always use the upstream proxy; direct domains stay direct unless a higher-priority proxy rule such as GFWList matches.

Status.ServiceRestarting: Service restart requested. The page will reconnect shortly.
Status.ServiceStopping: Service stop requested. The web console will become unavailable.
Status.ServiceUnavailableHint: The service is not reachable. Start it from the system service manager or command line, then refresh this page.
Status.ModeTunDescription: TUN transparently captures traffic and routes it by rule.
Status.TrafficChartTitle: Traffic trend for the last 30 minutes
Status.TrafficChartDescription: Sampled every {0} seconds. Blue is sent, cyan is received.
Status.Heading: Service Status
Status.Title: Status

Reason.ProxyDenied: Proxy temporarily denied CONNECT requests (for example HTTP 503).

Hint.ProxyDenied: TunProxy now uses a short cooldown and retries automatically. If the issue keeps repeating, open Config and run proxy check, then verify endpoint/credentials.
Hint.ConnectFailed: Check whether the proxy endpoint is reachable and whether local firewall or network policy blocks outbound access.
Hint.DnsFailed: Check upstream DNS connectivity. If DoH fallback succeeds, this warning should clear after new successful connections.
Hint.Generic: Review the error detail above and validate proxy settings in Config page.

Shared.RefreshAt: Auto refresh every {0} seconds | Last update: {1}

FixTun.CannotConnectApi:
Unable to connect to the service API: {0}

Please confirm the service is running.
FixTun.Menu: Repair: switch to TUN mode (configuration was overwritten)
FixTun.SuccessMessage: TUN mode has been written back to the configuration. The service will restart automatically shortly.

Install.PrerequisiteFailed: Cannot enable the TUN system service because enabled GEO/GFW resources are missing or invalid. Open Config, download the missing resources, then try again.
Install.SuccessMessage: Service installation completed. TUN mode has been enabled and the service was started automatically.

Tray.NoRunningCli: No running TunProxy CLI process was found.

Uninstall.ConfirmMessage:
Are you sure you want to uninstall the TunProxy system service?
After uninstalling, the app will switch back to Local Proxy mode.
